summary: Chris Riccomini announces the launch of Materialized View, a newsletter focused on software infrastructure. The newsletter originated from a Twitter thread sharing interesting infrastructure projects that gained unexpected traction. Rather than continuing to post scattered content across multiple platforms, Riccomini decided to consolidate his thoughts into a regular newsletter. The publication will cover infrastructure news, developer interviews, stack deep dives, and project highlights on a biweekly schedule.
key_points: Materialized View will cover software infrastructure news, developer interviews, stack deep dives, and project/paper highlights
The newsletter originated organically from a popular Twitter thread about infrastructure projects
Content will be published approximately every two weeks with 3-4 items per issue
The newsletter is free but premium content may be added based on reader interest
money_quotes: Rather than posting things scattershot on Twitter, Bluesky, Github, and in private DMs and texts, I'm going to share my thoughts publicly and regularly in one place: Materialized View.
The newsletter will go out like messages in a Kafka producer with a linger.ms of 2 weeks and a batch.size of 3-4 items.
key_insight: There's clear demand for curated, thoughtful coverage of software infrastructure that goes beyond scattered social media posts.
